title: Erigeron steudelii
taxonomy: Erigeron steudelii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. At the rank of order, it is also classified as a dicot. Further, it is classified as a flowering plant, with a specific type of vascular tissue. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as sunflowers and daisies.

preparations: Erigeron steudelii, also known as Steudel's fleabane, is a plant used in herbal medicine. The roots and leaves of this plant are typically used to prepare medicinal extracts. In traditional medicine, Erigeron steudelii is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. intro: Erigeron steudelii, also known as Steudel's fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to have potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron steudelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Erigeron steudelii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
